title: Fence validator accepts array-shaped region maps
reasoning: fenceArtifactStatus only checks that artifact.regions is a non-null object before casting it to a record. Arrays satisfy that runtime check, and validFenceRegions then uses Object.keys/Object.values, so a JSON array containing valid-looking region objects can be accepted as a usable fence artifact even though the expected contract is a named region map. This weakens the fail-closed safety gate because malformed/tampered artifact shape can be trusted.
reproduction: Write a fence-fidelity.json whose regions value is an array containing the same valid region objects used by the tests; the Object.values-based validation can still pass and return usable=true.
recommendation: Reject arrays for artifact.regions before calling validFenceRegions, for example by reusing isRecord(artifact.regions). Consider also requiring region names to be non-empty strings if region keys are part of the contract.
whyTestsDoNotAlreadyCoverThis: The fence tests cover missing files, valid object-shaped regions, stale SHA, bad metadata, tampered Wilson stats, and bad admissible flags, but they do not cover malformed container types such as regions arrays.
suggestedRegressionTest: Add a fenceArtifactStatus test that writes a valid fence artifact with regions converted from an object to an array of the same region rows and expects usable=false with reason invalid-regions or invalid-metadata.
minimumFixScope: packages/artifacts/src/main/artifacts.ts plus a focused fenceArtifactStatus regression test.
